column6: Please Add the Compound shown here in the Window Display: "kālāñjanī". This compound Deserves to be a Searchable Headword, located in the Hierarchy List Column.

Please Add the Four Compounds as shown here in the Window Display: "kālāntarāvṛta a. hidden or concealed in the womb of time." LB "kālāntarakṣama a. able to bear delay". LB "kālāntaraprekṣin a." LB "kālāntaraviṣaḥ an animal venomous only when enraged, as a rat." LB These compounds deserve to be Searchable Headwords, located in the Hierarchy List Column. Of Course an additional "Indentation" will be required following the pūrvapada: "kālāntara", in the Hierarchy List Column.

column7: Typo – Please note that this is a "Print Change", as confirmed by Andhrabharati here: https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/119#issuecomment-4389773140

Typo – Please note this correction is suggested because Frankly, the letter printed here as: " Ś " , is correct in itself !  The addition of the letter " h " , simply confuses things. If you choose to maintain the letter " h " , then the form should be changed to : " Sh ", which I would consider a "Downgrade", but BETTER than " Śh " , which is painful to my eyes !   What would the Master Himself have to say ?  Consider THAT !

column6: Since the Headword: "kālāyasam" is a Homonym with TWO Separate Listing Locations in the Hierarchy List Column, a Protocol similar to MW Dictionary should be employed by introducing the Numberings: "1" and "2", along with the ability to "CLICK" upon One or the Other Headword in the Window Display in order to be "Transported" to the Related Location within the Hiearchy List Column. ALSO, the respective Homonym Number should appear to the Right Side of each of the Two Headwords in the Hierarchy List Column as well, for ease of identification.
